steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers concluded that in only one of the three countries are there more insect species than fungus species posing risks to the trees. The correct choice must name that one country and show that the other two have more fungi than insects.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Great Britain and Belgium both reported fewer damaging insect species than damaging fungus species, whereas Bulgaria reported 16 fungus species and only 9 insect species.': Misrepresents Bulgaria — Bulgaria has 16 insects and 14 fungi, not 9 insects. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Great Britain reported 290 damaging fungus species, which is a greater number of them than either Bulgaria or Belgium reported.': Compares countries only on fungus counts — says nothing about the insect-vs.-fungus pattern the conclusion requires. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Bulgaria reported 14 damaging fungus species and 16 damaging insect species, while both Great Britain and Belgium reported a greater number of damaging fungus species than insect species.': Bulgaria has more insects (16) than fungi (14); Great Britain and Belgium each have more fungi than insects. This is precisely the "only one country" pattern the researchers described. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Great Britain reported 290 damaging fungus species, whereas Belgium reported 11 damaging insect species.': Isolated figures with no comparison between insects and fungi — cannot support the conclusion. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
